Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Polymethoxyflavone (PMF) biosynthesis within the flavone branch of flavonoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: High-value target for breeding citrus with elevated PMF content and enhanced health-promoting/anticancer properties; useful for PMF biofortification and metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: SAM-dependent O-methylation of multiple hydroxyl groups on hydroxyflavones; efficiently methylates mainly C6 and C4' positions and collaborates with CreOMT4/5 to generate fully O-methylated PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Chromosome_Position: Scaffold 4 within a 360-kb candidate region; exact coordinates NA
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: S-adenosylmethionine (SAM) methyl donor; pH optimum 7.5; temperature optimum 45 °C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of multiple citrus PMFs including nobiletin, sinensetin, tangeretin-related products and total PMFs in flavedo/callus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping, metabolome-transcriptome correlation, yeast feeding assays, recombinant protein enzyme assay, citrus callus overexpression, transient fruit flavedo overexpression, RNAi suppression
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Fruit flavedo, leaves, callus suspension culture, and fruit developmental stages; especially assessed around 60 DPA and in mandarin domestication contrast
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Recombinant CreOMT3 methylated hydroxyflavone substrates in yeast and purified enzyme assays; kinetic parameters determined for DMF1, 3'DNOB, GEN, CIR, LYS
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Expression highly correlated with total PMFs and six PMF monomers; OMT3 overexpression increased PMFs and RNAi reduced PMFs; acts on hydroxylated flavone intermediates in proposed terminal methylation steps
Pathway_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Functional CreOMT3 allele present in PMF-rich mandarins; specific favorable sequence variant not defined
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Overlapping BSA in two F1 populations plus transcriptome-metabolome correlation and candidate-gene cloning
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: F1 RT × TO population; F1 CLM × PTO population; 147-accession citrus panel; 72 mandarins for promoter-related genotyping context
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium overexpression/RNAi assays; in vitro enzyme assay conditions; no major abiotic treatment central to interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC for PMF profiling; chromatographic comparison to standards; integrated metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: One of a tandem OMT cluster; individually regioselective but complementary with CreOMT4/5; likely under purifying selection relative to wild mandarin ortholog comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA pool sequencing: BioProject PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533, PRJNA579049, PRJNA776912
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not established; domestication favored lower PMF in modern mandarins, but explicit agronomic tradeoff not demonstrated
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Multiple O-methylated hydroxyflavones and PMF intermediates/products including TMF2, API-TMF, SIN, NOB-related products
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including 5,6-OH-4',7-dimethoxyflavone (DMF1), 3'-demethylnobiletin, genkwanin, cirsimaritin, lysionotin and others
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant O-methyltransferase family with conserved PF08100 domain
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Transient overexpression increased total PMFs by average 11.6%; RNAi decreased total PMFs by average 12.9%; significant increases in TMF2 and 5DNOB upon OE and decreases of SIN/TMF1 under RNAi
Pathway_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: C. reticulata Clementine genome/scaffold assembly version NA
Pathway_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Product: Polymethoxyflavones (including nobiletin, sinensetin, tangeretin and related PMFs)
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Content/accumulation and composition of individual and total PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Fruit flavedo primarily; also leaves and callus
Pathway_Genes.Variety_or_Cultivar: Red tangerine; Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin; Clementine used for cloning/comparison

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Polymethoxyflavone (PMF) biosynthesis within the flavone branch of flavonoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Major candidate for marker-assisted selection and biofortification because promoter variation explains high versus low PMF content in mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: SAM-dependent multisite O-methylation of hydroxyflavones, preferentially at C7 and C5, and also capable of C3, C6 and C3' methylation depending on substrate; converts intermediates such as 5DNOB toward nobiletin
Pathway_Genes.Chromosome_Position: Scaffold 4 within OMT tandem cluster in 360-kb candidate region; exact coordinates NA
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: S-adenosylmethionine (SAM) methyl donor; pH optimum 7.5; temperature optimum 45 °C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes PMF accumulation; higher promoter activity allele associates with higher PMF content in wild and early-admixture mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping, transcript-metabolite correlation, yeast and purified recombinant enzyme assays, citrus callus overexpression, transient fruit overexpression, RNAi suppression, site-directed mutagenesis, dual-luciferase promoter assay, association/genotyping across mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Fruit flavedo and fruit development; domestication comparison among wild, early-admixture, and modern cultivated mandarins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Purified CreOMT4 methylated 3HF (C3), GAD (C5), DMF1 (C5/C6), LYS (C5/C7), 3'DNOB (C3'), GEN (C5), CIR (C5); kinetic constants reported
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Expression strongly correlated with total PMFs (r about 0.903) and six monomers; OE increased PMFs by 16.9% and RNAi reduced total PMFs by 14.0%; continuous conversion of 5DNOB to NOB in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Long/ancestral OMT4 promoter containing a 1,041-bp fragment with two MITEs
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: BSA fine candidate selection plus transcriptome-metabolome integration and functional cloning
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: F1 RT × TO; F1 CLM × PTO; 147 citrus accessions; 72 mandarins genotyped for promoter allele
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Promoter transactivation tested in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; transient Agrobacterium expression/RNAi in citrus flavedo
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: N17 residue essential for C3/C5/C6/C3' methylation; T168 modulates activity; 1,041-bp promoter fragment located ~654 bp upstream of ATG
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Type: Missense substitution and promoter deletion/MITE-containing insertion-presence polymorphism
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC profiling of PMFs; recombinant enzyme assays with chromatographic product identification; dual-LUC for promoter activity
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: CreOMT4 neofunctionalized relative to OMT6; N17D nearly abolished most activities, whereas CreOMT6-D17N gained multisite methylation capacity; promoter fragment gave ~3-fold higher LUC than short cultivated promoter
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA pool sequencing: PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533, PRJNA579049, PRJNA776912
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Modern domestication selected reduced-expression promoter associated with lower PMFs; direct yield/fitness tradeoff not shown
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: O-methylated hydroxyflavones and major PMFs such as NOB, SIN, TAN-related products
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including DMF1, 3HF, GAD, LYS, 3'DNOB, GEN, CIR and 5DNOB intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant O-methyltransferase family with conserved PF08100 domain
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: OE increased total PMFs by average 16.9%; RNAi reduced total PMFs by average 14.0%; CrmOMT4 long promoter produced ~3× LUC of Clementine short promoter; high PMF wild mandarins carry long promoter
Pathway_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: C. reticulata Clementine genome/scaffold assembly version NA
Pathway_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Product: Polymethoxyflavones, especially nobiletin, sinensetin, tangeretin, 5-demethylnobiletin-related flux
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Content/accumulation and compositional conversion among PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Fruit flavedo primarily; also callus and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Variety_or_Cultivar: Mangshan mandarin, Clementine mandarin, Red tangerine, Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin, Ota Ponkan mandarin

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Polymethoxyflavone (PMF) biosynthesis within the flavone branch of flavonoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful gene for increasing PMF content through breeding or metabolic engineering of citrus and heterologous production systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: SAM-dependent O-methylation of hydroxyflavones, with strong activity at C7, C5 and C3' positions and additional multisite methylation on select substrates, contributing to formation of fully methylated PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Chromosome_Position: Scaffold 4 within OMT tandem cluster in 360-kb candidate region; exact coordinates NA
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: S-adenosylmethionine (SAM) methyl donor; pH optimum 7.5; temperature optimum 45 °C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of multiple PMFs and strongly contributes to conversion of hydroxylated intermediates toward nobiletin and related end products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping, metabolome-transcriptome correlation, yeast feeding assays, purified recombinant enzyme assay, callus overexpression, transient fruit overexpression, RNAi suppression
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Fruit flavedo, leaves, callus, and developing mandarin fruit; domestication-associated PMF variation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Purified CreOMT5 methylated 3HF (C3), GAD (C5), DMF1 (C5/C6), LYS (C5/C7), 3'DNOB (C3'), CIR (C5); no activity detected on GEN
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Expression strongly correlated with total PMFs (r about 0.906); OE increased total PMFs by 14.0% and RNAi reduced by 18.6%; in vitro continuously converted 5DNOB to NOB
Pathway_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Functional CreOMT5 allele in PMF-rich mandarins; specific favorable variant not resolved
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: BSA candidate-gene identification plus transcriptome-metabolome integration and functional cloning
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: F1 RT × TO; F1 CLM × PTO; 147 citrus accessions
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium overexpression/RNAi in fruit flavedo; in vitro biochemical assay conditions
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC profiling and product verification with standards; enzyme kinetics with purified proteins
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Neofunctionalized relative to OMT6 by possessing C3-O-methylation activity; acts complementarily with CreOMT3 and CreOMT4
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA pool sequencing: PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533, PRJNA579049, PRJNA776912
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Multiple O-methylated flavone intermediates and major PMFs including NOB, SIN, TAN-related products
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including 3HF, GAD, DMF1, LYS, 3'DNOB, CIR, 5DNOB and others
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant O-methyltransferase family with conserved PF08100 domain
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Transient OE increased total PMFs by average 14.0%; RNAi reduced total PMFs by average 18.6%; significantly lowered 5DNOB in OE and reduced ISIN, NOB, TMF2, TAN, GAD, 5DNOB in RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: C. reticulata Clementine genome/scaffold assembly version NA
Pathway_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Product: Polymethoxyflavones including nobiletin and related PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Content/accumulation and conversion of PMF intermediates to major PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Fruit flavedo primarily; leaves and callus also analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Variety_or_Cultivar: Red tangerine; Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin; Clementine mandarin

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Polymethoxyflavone-related flavone O-methylation pathway; proposed ancestral precursor to mandarin PMF pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Evolutionary source gene for engineering neofunctionalized PMF OMTs; limited direct breeding value because expression/activity is weaker or less suitable in many citrus backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates selected hydroxyflavones, but generally weak or absent at C3, C5 and C7 for most citrus OMT6 homologs; functional capacity differs sharply from CreOMT4/5
Pathway_Genes.Chromosome_Position: Approximately 1.6 Mb downstream of OMT cluster in C. reticulata genome; exact coordinates NA
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Likely SAM-dependent O-methyltransferase; detailed assay conditions analogous to other OMTs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Does not explain major natural PMF variation despite expression correlation in Red tangerine; inferred ancestral enzyme from which OMT cluster duplicated and neofunctionalized
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cloning of OMT6 homologs from multiple citrus accessions, recombinant enzyme assays, expression-PMF correlation, comparative genomics/synteny, site-directed mutagenesis of D17N and A/T168 comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Fruit developmental stages and interspecific citrus comparison; domestication/evolutionary context
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Recombinant OMT6 proteins from multiple accessions were assayed on hydroxyflavones; CreOMT6 amino acid substitutions altered activity, with D17N conferring C3/C5/C6/C3' methylation capacity
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Not associated with PMF content by correlation table, but homologous to OMT cluster and biochemically active on some substrates; positioned as ancestral source prior to OMT cluster expansion
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Comparative genomics, synteny analysis, candidate cloning from multiple citrus accessions, enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Comparative panel of citrus groups I-IV; expression analyses in Red tangerine development and multiple citrus accessions
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Evolutionary/species background is critical; no external treatment central
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: Residue D17 in CreOMT6; D17N mutation enabled C3/C5/C6/C3' methylation; residue corresponding to T168/A168 also tested
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC-based enzyme product profiling; gene expression correlation with PMF levels
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Highly similar to CreOMT3/4/5 (91.2-95.5% identity) and considered the probable source of tandem duplication; most OMT6 homologs outside mandarin group lack strong PMF-forming activity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA341533, PRJNA579049, PRJNA776912; BSA sequencing context PRJNA938971
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Ancestral flavone O-methylation module preceding specialized PMF branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Limited O-methylated hydroxyflavones; exact dominant products for CreOMT6 not fully specified
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones; substrate panel similar to OMT cluster assays
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant O-methyltransferase family; PF08100-containing homolog closely linked to OMT cluster
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No direct OE/RNAi effect reported; D17N mutation conferred gain of C3/C5/C6/C3' methylation activity
Pathway_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: C. reticulata genome/scaffold assembly version NA
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Pathway capacity and enzyme activity, rather than clear quantitative PMF accumulation effect
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Fruit flavedo and developmental samples for expression; in vitro assays for metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Variety_or_Cultivar: Red tangerine and comparative citrus accessions

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ancestral flavone O-methylation pathway related to PMF biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential donor enzyme for metabolic engineering, but low direct breeding utility because it is not expressed in primitive citrus and does not itself underpin PMF-rich phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Robustly O-methylates eight hydroxyflavone substrates at C5, C6, C7, C3' and C4' positions, but unlike CreOMT4/5 lacks C3 O-methylation neofunctionalization
Pathway_Genes.Chromosome_Position: Genome location in Atalantia buxfoliata exact coordinates NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Biochemically competent OMT but negligible expression in primitive citrus means little/no PMF accumulation in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning from A. buxfoliata, recombinant protein assays across substrate panel, comparative expression analysis across citrus groups
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Primitive citrus species comparison; evolutionary context of pathway emergence
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Recombinant AbOMT6 methylated eight different hydroxyflavone substrates at multiple positions with robust activity similar to CreOMT4/5 on some reactions
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Orthologous/collinear with citrus OMT6 genes and placed at the base of phylogeny; not expressed in A. buxfoliata, explaining lack of PMF accumulation despite catalytic capacity
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Comparative genomics and ortholog cloning from primitive citrus species
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Comparative citrus species panel including Group I primitive citrus
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Species-specific expression background is critical; no treatment factor central
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: No specific favorable variant reported; sequence shares 94.9% identity with CreOMT6
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Recombinant enzyme assays with HPLC product profiling
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Probably most ancient gene related to OMT cluster and CreOMT6; supports model that enzymatic capability predated expression/neofunctionalization events that generated mandarin PMF pathway
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative expression datasets include RNA-seq BioProjects PRJNA341533, PRJNA579049, PRJNA776912
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant O-methyltransferase family; ortholog of OMT6
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In vivo PMF accumulation negligible in A. buxfoliata because gene expression was not detected/negligible
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Enzymatic O-methylation capacity versus negligible in vivo accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Comparative citrus tissues including fruit flavedo; in vitro substrate assays

Title: Neofunctionalization of an OMT cluster dominates polymethoxyflavone biosynthesis associated with the domestication of citrus
